check.name: backend-build
check.note: Build succeeded. Warnings remain for System.Security.Cryptography.Pkcs 6.0.0 (NU1903).

check.name: frontend-build
check.note: Build succeeded (vite v7.3.1, 2181 modules, 17s). Chunk size warning only.

ticket.finding: All file inputs now have accept attributes matching the backend AllowedExtensions policy.
ticket.evidence: Added accept attribute to file input.
Added accept attribute to file input.
Already had accept attribute.
Already had accept attribute.
Already had accept attribute.
Already had accept attribute.

ticket.finding: Workspace deletion now soft-deletes direct documents and immediate child workspaces, but it is not recursive and can still orphan documents inside deleted child workspaces.
ticket.evidence: DeleteWorkspaceAsync soft-deletes documents whose WorkspaceId matches the deleted workspace.
DeleteWorkspaceAsync also soft-deletes immediate child workspaces.
ticket.nextAction: Make the delete cascade recursive or explicitly walk descendants and soft-delete their documents too.

ticket.finding: Task completion now blocks actions when the referenced workspace is missing or deleted.
ticket.evidence: CompleteTaskAsync checks task.WorkspaceId before processing.
The method stops when the workspace lookup returns null or IsDeleted is true.
ticket.nextAction: Regression-test approve/reject/complete against deleted workspaces and deleted parent-workspace scenarios.

ticket.finding: Workspace storage limits are now enforced during upload.
ticket.evidence: UploadDocumentAsync checks workspace.Settings.StorageLimitMb.
The method converts the configured limit to bytes and compares it to current usage plus the incoming file.
The upload returns a storage limit exceeded response when the limit would be breached.

ticket.finding: The 401/403 interceptor route allowlist now covers all auth entry points: /login, /login/admin, /login/adminP, /registration, /forgot-password, /reset-password.
ticket.evidence: authPages array includes all auth routes for 401 handling.
authPages array includes all auth routes for 403 handling.
ticket.nextAction: Refresh-token mechanism is still absent — consider implementing for production hardening.

ticket.finding: Workflow CRUD activity logging was added, but trigger evaluation and workflow-instance operations still have audit gaps.
ticket.evidence: CreateAsync now writes an Activity record.
UpdateAsync now writes an Activity record.
DeleteAsync now writes an Activity record.
EvaluateTriggersAsync exists, but there is no matching Activities.AddAsync call in that path before StartWorkflowAsync is invoked.
EvaluateConfidenceAsync still contains no LogActivity call.
StartStepAsync still contains no LogActivity call.
ticket.nextAction: Finish the missing Activity coverage for trigger evaluation, confidence-based branching, step starts, and termination paths.

ticket.finding: The integration default was changed from Active to Final, but status handling is still inconsistent across the upload path and the rest of Vault largely expects Active/Draft/Pending.
ticket.evidence: GRC upload DTOs now default Status to Final.
The returned summary still falls back to Active.
DocumentService still persists dto.Status or Draft when no blocking workflow exists.
The core document model still defaults Status to Active.
ticket.nextAction: Define an explicit GRC-to-Vault status map and normalize both persistence and UI expectations around the same allowed values.

ticket.finding: Name updates still proxy to GRC and mirror fields locally, but there is no visible name-change detection, deactivation workflow, or authorization-letter trigger logic.
ticket.evidence: UpdateExternalUserAsync is a straight external PUT wrapper.
UserService forwards the external update call.
UserService then mirrors NameAr/NameEn locally without triggering any extra business flow.
ticket.nextAction: Introduce explicit old-vs-new name comparison and wire the required deactivation/auth-letter business process.

ticket.finding: The workflow editor is still configured for a single trigger selection on the start edge.
ticket.evidence: WorkflowTriggerBuilder is still passed singleSelect=true.
WorkflowTriggerBuilder has logic to enforce single-select mode when that prop is true.
ticket.nextAction: Clarify the intended product behavior first, then either switch to multi-select or keep the current behavior intentionally.

ticket.finding: A visible Trash2 delete button is now rendered next to the close button in the edge-properties panel header.
ticket.evidence: Edge properties title bar now contains a Trash2 button that removes the edge from state and deselects it.
Added deleteConnection translation key in both AR and EN.

ticket.finding: Task actions are still immediately actionable without a required preview step.
ticket.evidence: The modal exposes View Workspace/View Document as an optional link.
Reject remains enabled unless loading is true.
Approve remains enabled unless loading is true.
ticket.nextAction: Add a preview-confirmed flag or a stronger confirmation gate before action buttons become available.

ticket.finding: TokenAuth now preserves language from query string or token and applies it to storage and document direction.
ticket.evidence: TokenAuth reads lang from the query string.
TokenAuth reads lang/Language from the decoded token.
TokenAuth writes the resolved language into localStorage and updates the document language/direction.

ticket.finding: The TokenAuth transition screen has been reworked toward Musahm branding and localization, although this pass did not include a visual browser check.
ticket.evidence: The page now defines brand-aligned styling around Musahm gold (#c3924d).
The component is language-aware and renders based on the resolved locale.
ticket.nextAction: Do a quick browser comparison against the design system before treating this ticket as fully done.

ticket.finding: Login now keeps a local isSubmitting state, but the global loader still depends only on requestCount, so the original blank-gap problem can still happen between sequential auth requests and navigation.
ticket.evidence: Login now tracks isSubmitting explicitly.
The submit button reflects isSubmitting, but that state only affects the local page UI.
The global Loader only reads isLoading from LoadingContext.
isLoading is derived solely from requestCount > 0.
The loader is still request-based via interceptor increment/decrement calls.
ticket.nextAction: Either drive the global loader from the full login workflow state or keep a dedicated auth-transition overlay mounted until navigation completes.

ticket.finding: The workflow name and description inputs are now always visible above the conditional edge/node properties panels.
ticket.evidence: Workflow settings (name + description) are rendered in a separate always-visible we-properties div before the conditional ternary.

ticket.finding: The storage-limit input now allows empty intermediate state and coerces to a number only on submit.
ticket.evidence: State type changed to number|string, onChange allows empty string, submit uses Number() || 0.

ticket.finding: The current pass did not locate the exact Rules and Exceptions modal implementation quickly enough to re-verify the reported checkbox-spacing issue from code alone.
ticket.nextAction: Locate the exact modal component, inspect the checkbox/label spacing styles in-browser, and then decide whether the fix is CSS-only or a design-spec mismatch.

ticket.finding: The start-edge field label now uses the specific t('connectionName') key instead of t('name').
ticket.evidence: Label changed from t('name') to t('connectionName').
Added connectionName: "Connection Name" (EN) and connectionName: "اسم الرابط" (AR).
